question.text: What does KIM stand for in mutual funds?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** KIM stands for Key Information Memorandum. It is a short summary document that gives investors a quick overview of the scheme. Think of it as a "one-page summary" of the SID.
**The Trap:** KIM is not the same as the full SID. It only has key highlights. For complete details, one must refer to the SID.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. Remember the hierarchy: SID (full details) > SAI (AMC info) > KIM (quick summary).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** KIM is typically given to investors at the time of purchase along with the application form. It is handy for a quick reference.
